description: For sales, retail, customer service, and frontline roles. Emphasises people skills, resilience, results, and customer focus.

examples.title: Sales Assistant Application at Shoprite
examples.body:
6 March 2026

Dear Store Manager,

I am writing to apply for the Sales Assistant position at your Shoprite store. I am a recent high school graduate who is hardworking, friendly, and eager to start my career in retail.

Although I do not have formal work experience, I have developed skills that are directly relevant to retail. At school, I was the class treasurer for three years, which taught me cash handling, record-keeping, and accountability. I also organised the annual school fun day, where I negotiated with vendors, managed a budget of ZMW 2,000, and sold tickets to over 300 students and parents. That experience taught me that sales is about enthusiasm, clarity, and making the customer feel good about their purchase.

I am a natural communicator who enjoys talking to people. I am patient, which helps me handle difficult customers calmly. I am also physically fit and willing to work long hours on my feet, restocking shelves, assisting customers, and maintaining store cleanliness.

I understand that retail is the frontline of customer experience. Every interaction shapes whether a customer returns. I am committed to representing your store with professionalism, courtesy, and a positive attitude.

I am available to start immediately and willing to work any shift, including weekends and holidays.

Yours sincerely,

Reuben Kalaba

examples.title: Customer Service Representative at Airtel Retail
examples.body:
19 July 2026

Dear Recruitment Manager,

I am applying for the Customer Service Representative position at your Airtel retail centre. I am a diploma holder in Business Administration from Evelyn Hone College, and I am passionate about delivering excellent customer experiences.

During my attachment at a mobile money agency, I served over 100 customers per day, handling transactions, troubleshooting failed transfers, and explaining service options. I learned that customer service is not about having all the answers, it is about listening, empathising, and finding solutions. I developed a personal system: greet every customer by name when possible, listen fully before responding, explain clearly without jargon, and confirm that the issue is resolved before the customer leaves.

I also handled complaints. When a customer's airtime purchase failed three times, I stayed calm, apologised sincerely, processed a manual refund, and gave them a bonus data bundle as a gesture of goodwill. The customer later returned to thank me personally and became a regular. That taught me that turning a complaint into a positive experience creates the most loyal customers.

I am tech-savvy, quick to learn new systems, and fluent in English, Nyanja, and Bemba. I believe that language matters in customer service, customers are more comfortable when they can express themselves in their preferred language.

I am eager to bring my customer service skills, positive energy, and problem-solving abilities to your team.

Best regards,

Rosemary Chola

examples.title: Field Sales Representative for FMCG Company
examples.body:
25 September 2026

Dear Sales Manager,

I am writing to apply for the Field Sales Representative position. I am a recent marketing graduate from Copperbelt University, and I have the energy, resilience, and results focus that field sales demands.

During my studies, I worked part-time as a promoter for a beverage company, visiting small shops and kiosks in Kitwe to introduce new products. I had to approach strangers, pitch products in 30 seconds, handle rejections, and close sales. In my best month, I signed 28 new outlets and exceeded my target by 40%. The experience taught me that field sales is a numbers game, consistency matters more than any single interaction.

I am physically resilient and comfortable with travel. I understand that field sales involves long days, dusty roads, and unpredictable weather. I am also organised, I keep detailed records of every outlet visited, every conversation, and every follow-up required. I believe that good salespeople are good organisers.

I am a quick learner who can master product knowledge rapidly. I am also competitive in a healthy way, I set personal targets above my assigned ones and celebrate small wins to stay motivated.

I am excited about the opportunity to build relationships with retailers, grow your product's distribution, and earn based on results.

Yours sincerely,

William Mwape
